_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting security assessments of data platforms and AI systems, including vulnerability identification, penetration testing coordination, and security architecture review.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. Skill SP-S-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.438), SP-K-001 (SP, cosine: 0.437).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.9/10 (raw 0.587). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [cases, cybersecurity, develop, use] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, architecture, assessments, conducting, coordination, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.856 (P=0.868, R=0.844). P exceeds R by 0.023 — the NICE element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 21 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). "Develop cybersecurity use cases" and "Skill in conducting security assessments of data platforms and AI systems, inclu" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.457 < STS 0.587 (gap -0.130). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [cases, cybersecurity, develop] and [ai, architecture, assessments] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [cases, cybersecurity, develop, use]. WIDAI terms: [ai, architecture, assessments, conducting, coordination, data].
